chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ حَثَا عَلَى الْمَيِّتِ وَ كَيْفَ يُحْثَى
chapter_title.en: Chapter 66 – The one who spreads (soil) upon the deceased, and how he should spread it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/104)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/104)
text: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَا مَا شَاءَ النَّاسُ فَلَمَّا انْتَهَى إِلَى الْقَبْرِ تَنَحَّى فَجَلَسَ فَلَمَّا أُدْخِلَ الْمَيِّتُ لَحْدَهُ قَامَ فَحَثَا عَلَيْهِ التُّرَابَ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ بِيَدِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Dawood Bin Al Numan who said,

‘I saw Abu Al Hassanasws saying: ‘Whatever Allahazwj so Desires and not what the people desire’. So when heasws ended up to the grave, heasws isolated himselfasws and sat down. So when the deceased was entered into his chasm (Lahad), heasws stood up and spread the soil upon him, three times, by hisasws hand’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Dawud ibn al-Nu‘man who has said the following: “I heard abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘It is what Allah wills, not what people will.’ When he approached the grave, he kept to a side and sat down. When the deceased was placed in the grave, he stood up and filled three handfuls of soil in the grave, one at a time.”
